title: cn() does not dedupe conflicting prefixes
prompt:
src/cn.ts joins CSS class strings but does NOT resolve conflicts. When two tokens share a prefix from CONFLICT_PREFIXES (e.g., both start with `bg-`), only the LAST occurrence should remain — the earlier one must be dropped. Non-conflicting classes keep their original relative order.

Examples:
  cn("bg-red-500", "bg-blue-500")  →  "bg-blue-500"
  cn("bg-red-500 font-bold", "bg-blue-500")  →  "font-bold bg-blue-500"
  cn("bg-red-500 text-sm p-2", "bg-blue-500 text-lg")  →  "p-2 bg-blue-500 text-lg"

Fix the cn() function so the failing tests in tests/target.test.ts pass. The tests in tests/regression.test.ts (basic concatenation, undefined handling, whitespace collapse) must continue to pass.

Do not modify the tests. Do not add new files. Use the existing CONFLICT_PREFIXES + prefixOf helper.
